mnemonics.title: GREEN IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: Remember the 3 pillars: EPE - Environment, Profit, Ethics. Think: 'Eco-friendly Products Earn Profits'.

mnemonics.title: Green IT Strategies
mnemonics.content: VRESS - Virtualization, Renewable energy, Energy efficiency, Sustainable sourcing, Solid waste management. Sentence: 'Very Responsible Enterprises Save Sustainability'.

mnemonics.title: E-waste Hazards
mnemonics.content: LeMPB - Lead (brain damage), Mercury (kidney), Cadmium (bones), Chromium (cancer). 'Lead Makes People Badly Crushed'.

mnemonics.title: LCA Stages
mnemonics.content: MRDUD - Material extraction, Manufacturing, Distribution, Use, Disposal. 'My Robot Drives Underwater Daily'.

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency; lower is better (ideal = 1.0)
Inverse of PUE expressed as percentage; DCiE = 1/PUE × 100
Measures carbon emissions per unit of IT energy consumed
Measures water consumption for data center cooling
Rating system for environmental performance of electronics
EU directive limiting toxic materials in electronics
EU directive for proper e-waste disposal and recycling
Method to evaluate environmental impacts throughout product lifecycle
Policy approach where manufacturers take responsibility for end-of-life products
Businesses' responsibility to contribute to sustainable development

visualTips.visualization: Think of PUE as a ratio: imagine a pie chart where IT equipment gets the 'useful' slice and everything else (cooling, lighting, etc.) is 'wasted' energy. The smaller the non-IT slice, the closer PUE is to 1.0.
Visualize a three-legged stool - remove any leg (Environmental, Social, Economic) and the stool falls. Green IT needs all three pillars to be sustainable.
Imagine one big apartment building (physical server) with many small apartments (virtual servers) instead of many small houses (individual physical servers). Same space, more units!
Think of a pyramid: Top (best) = Reduce/Reuse, Middle = Recycle, Bottom (worst) = Dispose. Always try from top down.
Hot aisle/cold aisle containment - imagine traffic lanes: hot air flows one direction, cold air the opposite, like separated highways for temperature.

analogies.analogy: Just like a city needs power plants, water treatment, and waste management, a data center needs energy supply, cooling systems, and waste heat management - all requiring resources.
Server virtualization is like carpooling - instead of one person per car (one app per server), multiple people share the same vehicle (apps share the server), reducing fuel (energy) consumption.
Improper e-waste disposal is like a ticking time bomb - the toxic materials slowly leak into the environment, causing long-term damage like a slow-motion explosion.
Cloud computing is like the electrical power grid - instead of every home having its own generator (local servers), we share centralized efficient power plants (cloud data centers).

Green IT is like recycling for technology - just as we recycle paper and plastic to reduce environmental impact, we 'recycle' computing resources through virtualization and reuse hardware to minimize IT's carbon footprint.
